semantic.role: Scala AST extraction rule configuration for ArchSpine
responsibilities: Define patterns for identifying Scala imports, exports (functions, classes, objects, traits, etc.), and usages (calls, property access) in source code.
Provide the mapping of Scala language constructs to abstract syntax tree (AST) node kinds for analysis and indexing.
outOfScope: Other programming languages or their AST rules.
Runtime execution or semantic analysis beyond syntax extraction.

English.purpose: This document is the Scala language rule set for the ArchSpine AST extraction engine. It defines the syntactic patterns that the scanner uses to parse Scala source files, capturing imports, exported symbols (like functions, classes, traits), and usage references. These rules enable ArchSpine to build an accurate symbol index and dependency graph for Scala projects.
English.context_and_audience: This document is intended for developers maintaining or extending the ArchSpine codebase, particularly those working on multi-language support or contributing to the AST extractor for the Scala language. It assumes familiarity with both ArchSpine's scanning architecture and Scala syntax.
key_takeaways: The rule file covers three categories: imports, exports, and usages.
Export patterns include functions (public and private), classes, case classes, objects, traits, sealed traits, values (val/var), and type aliases.
Usages capture function calls and property access via dot notation.
These patterns directly influence the quality of the knowledge graph and views generated by ArchSpine.
The rules are written in a custom YAML syntax specific to the ArchSpine scanner engine.
